From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Estonian runner and miner

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bd/Vjat%C5%A1eslav_Ko%C5%A1elev%2C_2010.jpg/250px-Vjat%C5%A1eslav_Ko%C5%A1elev%2C_2010.jpg)](/wiki/File:Vjat%C5%A1eslav_Ko%C5%A1elev,_2010.jpg)

Vjatšeslav Košelev

**Vjatšeslav Košelev** (born 20 January 1968) is a [Russian](/wiki/Russia "Russia")-born [Estonian](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") long distance runner, hurdler and miner.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

He was born in [Sverdlovsk](/wiki/Sverdlovsk,_Russia "Sverdlovsk, Russia"), Russia. In 1987, he graduated from Sverdlovsk polytechnical school.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

He started his athletics exercising in 1999, coached by Jevgeni Terentjev. In 1987 he won silver medal at [European Junior Athletics Championships](/wiki/European_Junior_Athletics_Championships "European Junior Athletics Championships") in the steeplechase running. He is 5-times Estonian champion in different running disciplines.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

Since 1992 he is working as a miner in a quarry called "Estonia".[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)
